data.title: What's Happening With Rigetti Computing Stock Thursday?
data.description:
Shares of Rigetti Computing, Inc. (NASDAQ:RGTI) are rising Thursday after B. Riley Securities upgraded the stock from a neutral to a buy while maintaining a $35 price target.

  • Rigetti Computing shares are powering higher. What’s fueling RGTI momentum?

Positivity For Near-Term Trajectory

B. Riley analyst Craig Ellis said the firm is encouraged with Rigetti’s commercial trajectory following a recent $8.4 million order, which comprises 48% and 36% of the consensus calendar year 2025 low- and high-end consensus range.

Also, the quantum computing sector overall is seeing some positive movement, the analyst noted.

September's Washington DC-based Quantum World Congress drew an estimated 1,000 participants, well-illustrating a rapidly advancing US-friendly-country quantum ecosystem. Laurence Berkeley National Labs is also undergoing workload migration validation, meaning that an evolution from tech research to commercialization may yield contract awards as funding approves.

Additionally, RGTI's market expanded into U.S. academic and international government deals with early-2026 follow-through. Further commercial, U.S. National Labs, U.S. Space-Force aligned AFRL and international government contract signings are in focus through the year.

High Risks For Rigetti Computing

Ellis said Rigetti holds and develops large quantities of protected IP in the forms of patents, copyright, trademarks and trade secrets. The company also has a history of operating losses, which management expects to continue into the future.

Rigetti projects that it will need an additional cash raise to continue its current rate of R&D spending, he noted.

Craig also said the full commercial value of quantum computing is undetermined, and that it’s unclear whether Rigetti's pursued implementation mechanism is an optimal implementation.

More than 80% of the company’s revenue in 2023 was from U.S. government entities, which means that the company is exposed to policy or budget reductions. Its reliance on cloud providers is also not optimal, according to Ellis, as they may prematurely terminate contracts, raise fees or limit available scale.

Rigetti Stock Rises

RGTI Price Action: Rigetti Computing shares were up 4.94% at $24.84 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock.com

data.title: Plug Power Stock Surges Ahead Of High-Stakes Reddit AMA
data.description:
Plug Power Inc (NASDAQ:PLUG) stock is trading higher Thursday afternoon as investors position ahead of a Q&A with longtime CEO Andy Marsh.

The Reddit Inc (NYSE:RDDT) "Ask Me Anything" is set for Thursday from 2:00–3:00 p.m. ET on the r/plugpowerstock forum and will focus on a Jan. 29 special shareholder meeting that could reshape the hydrogen specialist's capital structure. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Plug Power shares are powering higher. What’s behind PLUG gains?

Plug Power's Evolution In The Hydrogen Economy

Founded in the late 1990s, Plug Power has evolved from a niche fuel-cell maker into a vertically integrated green-hydrogen platform, offering electrolyzers, liquid hydrogen, fuel-cell systems, storage and fueling infrastructure.

The company has deployed over 72,000 fuel-cell systems and 285 fueling stations, operates hydrogen plants in Georgia, Tennessee and Louisiana capable of producing 40 tons per day, and counts Walmart, Amazon, Home Depot, BMW and BP among its customers.

Marsh, CEO since 2008, is slated to become executive chairman in March 2026, when Jose Luis Crespo takes over as chief executive.

Why Thursday’s AMA Matters For PLUG Investors

The special meeting asks shareholders to modernize voting standards to align with Delaware law and to increase authorized common shares after a similar proposal won about 84% of votes cast at the 2025 annual meeting but failed under stricter outstanding-share rules.

Approval would give Plug flexibility to raise equity, refinance obligations and fund growth projects. Rejection would likely force a reverse stock split, with potential liquidity and volatility risks.

For investors, today's AMA is a real-time read-through on sentiment around dilution vs. reverse split, funding runway for Plug's hydrogen build-out and confidence in the upcoming leadership transition.

Bullish Momentum For PLUG Stock

The stock is currently trading 12.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 3.2% above its 100-day SMA, indicating short-term strength. Shares have increased approximately 21.92% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, suggesting potential challenges ahead.

The RSI is at 48.82, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $2.50

The Earnings Report That Could Shift Sentiment

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on Mar. 2.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 11 cents (Up from $-1.65 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $218.10 million (Up from $191.47 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $2.38. Recent analyst moves include:

  • TD Cowen: Downgraded to Hold (Lowers Target to $2.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Clear Street: Upgraded to Buy (Lowers Target to $3.00) (Dec. 31, 2025)
  • Canaccord Genuity: Hold (Maintains Target to $2.50) (Nov. 20, 2025)

PLUG Stock Soars Thursday

PLUG Price Action: Plug Power shares were up 15.77% at $2.56 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: From Meme To Markets: Dogecoin's First SEC-Approved ETF Goes Live
data.description:
Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) has taken another step into the financial mainstream with the launch of a spot ETF backed by the Dogecoin Foundation. The 21shares Dogecoin ETF (NASDAQ:TDOG) began trading on Thursday, offering investors exposure to the meme-born cryptocurrency through traditional brokerage accounts.

TDOG is also the only spot Dogecoin ETF endorsed by the Dogecoin Foundation, the nonprofit that has supported the token's decentralized development since 2014. That endorsement comes through House of Doge, the foundation's relatively new corporate arm, backed by Elon Musk's personal attorney, Alex Spiro.

The ETF allows both retail and institutional investors to track Dogecoin's price without dealing with crypto wallets, private keys, or exchanges. TDOG is fully backed on a 1:1 basis by DOGE held in institutional-grade custody and carries a management fee of 0.50%.

A First For SEC-Approved Dogecoin Exposure

While Dogecoin ETFs are not entirely new, TDOG breaks important ground. Spot DOGE ETFs from Grayscale and Bitwise launched in November, but neither received explicit approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Those products went live through an automated process following a government shutdown.

Earlier this month, the SEC formally approved the 21Shares ETF, making TDOG the first spot Dogecoin ETF to receive a clear regulatory sign-off. In doing so, the agency effectively concluded for the first time that Dogecoin is not a security, a determination that could shape how the asset is treated going forward.

House of Doge has been working to move Dogecoin beyond its origins as an intentionally utility-free joke, pitching it instead as a potential global payment rail. The ETF launch fits into that broader effort to place Dogecoin inside established financial infrastructure.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Tariff Shockwaves Keeps EV And AI In Focus— Tradr Responds With Four New Inverse ETFs
data.description:
Renewed tariff tensions between the U.S. and the European Union have once again injected uncertainty into global markets, amplifying volatility in high-growth AI infrastructure and electric vehicle stocks. These sectors, already sensitive to macro shocks, have seen sharp price swings as investors attempt to price in trade risks alongside slowing global demand signals.

Against this backdrop, Tradr ETFs introduced four leveraged inverse products: the Tradr 2X Short APLD Daily ETF (BATS:APLZ), Tradr 2X Short IREN Daily ETF (BATS:IREZ), Tradr 2X Short LCID Daily ETF (BATS:LCIZ), and Tradr 2X Short NBIS Daily ETF (BATS:NBIZ), on Thursday. As the names suggest, the funds are tied inversely to Applied Digital Corp (NASDAQ:APLD), Iren Ltd (NASDAQ:IREN), Lucid Group Inc (NASDAQ:LCID), and Nebius Group NV (NASDAQ:NBIS), respectively.

"If you look back to last October when the AI infrastructure stocks were trading at all-time highs, you naturally saw short interest in these names begin to climb," Matt Markiewicz, Head of Product & Capital Markets at Tradr ETFs, told Benzinga. "That was definitely a gauge we were watching in terms of what the appetite could look like if we were to launch short funds on some of these stocks."

Why These Stocks Attracted Inverse Exposure

Tradr's selection process focused less on fundamentals and more on technical and sentiment signals, particularly after extended rallies tied to AI and EV optimism.

"We were fortunate to ride the price wave up in our leveraged long names on Applied Digital and Nebius," Markiewicz said, adding that investor demand remained strong even when the space pulled back in mid-October. "That's kind of when you sit back and realize that maybe it would be a good idea to have a short tool just in case the other side of the trade was looking for a home."

Recent stock rebounds may appear to challenge the logic of launching short products, but Markiewicz said that framing misses the point.

"We never launch products trying to time the market," he said. He added that the funds are designed for expressing short-term bearish views, particularly as investors reassess where AI and EV stocks stand in their respective cycles.

Trade tensions have only deepened uncertainty, reinforcing the case for tactical tools rather than long-term bets.

"I wish we had these funds out a week or two ago, but again you can't time these launches," Markiewicz said. "The macro picture is so cloudy there is no way we can really try to synchronize a launch with what is going on out there."

Built For Tactical Trades, Not Buy-And-Hold

Markiewicz emphasized that the new ETFs are intended for short-term positioning, such as around earnings or technical inflection points.

"You really don't want to be holding on to daily reset products for too long," he said, pointing to the risk of sharp price dislocations.

While leveraged ETFs compete with options and margin trading, he noted a key distinction: "With ETFs you can only lose what you put in."

Challenging ‘Leverage Is Bad' Thinking

As inverse and leveraged ETFs attract more attention, Markiewicz pushed back against broad-brush criticism of leverage.

"Yes, leverage could be dangerous if used incorrectly," he said, "but for investors who can stay on top of their trades, these products can be a powerful addition to a portfolio."

In a market defined by tariffs, geopolitics, and sudden reversals, that message is likely to resonate with traders navigating the current wave of volatility.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Looking At Unity Software's Recent Unusual Options Activity
data.description:
Deep-pocketed investors have adopted a bearish approach towards Unity Software (NYSE:U), and it's something market players shouldn't ignore. Our tracking of public options records at Benzinga unveiled this significant move today. The identity of these investors remains unknown, but such a substantial move in U usually suggests something big is about to happen.

We gleaned this information from our observations today when Benzinga's options scanner highlighted 11 extraordinary options activities for Unity Software. This level of activity is out of the ordinary.

The general mood among these heavyweight investors is divided, with 27% leaning bullish and 45% bearish. Among these notable options, 4 are puts, totaling $233,380, and 7 are calls, amounting to $854,864.

Expected Price Movements

Analyzing the Volume and Open Interest in these contracts, it seems that the big players have been eyeing a price window from $40.0 to $47.0 for Unity Software during the past quarter.

Volume & Open Interest Trends

Assessing the volume and open interest is a strategic step in options trading. These metrics shed light on the liquidity and investor interest in Unity Software's options at specified strike prices. The forthcoming data visualizes the fluctuation in volume and open interest for both calls and puts, linked to Unity Software's substantial trades, within a strike price spectrum from $40.0 to $47.0 over the preceding 30 days.

Unity Software Call and Put Volume: 30-Day Overview

Noteworthy Options Activity:

About Unity Software

Unity Software Inc provides a software platform for creating and operating interactive, real-time 3D content. The platform can be used to create, run, and monetize interactive, real-time 2D and 3D content for mobile phones, tablets, PCs, consoles, and augmented and virtual reality devices. The business is spread across the United States, Greater China, EMEA, APAC, and other Americas, and key revenue is derived from the EMEA region. The products are used in the gaming industry, retail, automotive, architecture, engineering, and construction.

Following our analysis of the options activities associated with Unity Software, we pivot to a closer look at the company's own performance.

Unity Software's Current Market Status

  • With a trading volume of 1,701,345, the price of U is up by 0.93%, reaching $42.38.
  • Current RSI values indicate that the stock is is currently neutral between overbought and oversold.
  • Next earnings report is scheduled for 20 days from now.

What The Experts Say On Unity Software

A total of 5 professional analysts have given their take on this stock in the last 30 days, setting an average price target of $52.0.

  • An analyst from Morgan Stanley persists with their Overweight rating on Unity Software, maintaining a target price of $52.
  • Consistent in their evaluation, an analyst from Jefferies keeps a Buy rating on Unity Software with a target price of $55.
  • Maintaining their stance, an analyst from Goldman Sachs continues to hold a Neutral rating for Unity Software, targeting a price of $47.
  • Consistent in their evaluation, an analyst from Wells Fargo keeps a Overweight rating on Unity Software with a target price of $54.
  • An analyst from Freedom Capital Markets downgraded its action to Buy with a price target of $52.

Options trading presents higher risks and potential rewards. Astute traders manage these risks by continually educating themselves, adapting their strategies, monitoring multiple indicators, and keeping a close eye on market movements.
